_readme: Seed for the Practice module. POST this whole object to /api/admin/practice/problems with an admin bearer token. DSA problems are judged via Piston (stdin -> stdout, compared with normalizeOutput). The SQL problem is judged in-app (sql.js) against expectedColumns/expectedRows. See seed/README-practice-seed.md for the curl command.

problems.title: Sum of an Array
problems.statementHtml:
You are given an array of n integers. Print the sum of all elements.

Input
Line 1: an integer n.
Line 2: n space-separated integers.

Output
A single integer — the sum.

starters.code:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    n = int(data[0])
    arr = list(map(int, data[1:1+n]))
    # TODO: print the sum

main()

hints.text: You only need to look at each element once.
Keep a running total and add each element to it.

problems.editorialHtml: Iterate once, accumulate. O(n) time, O(1) extra space.

solutions.code:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().split()
n = int(data[0])
print(sum(map(int, data[1:1+n])))

problems.title: Reverse a String
problems.statementHtml: Given a single line containing a string s (no spaces), print it reversed.

starters.code:
s = input().strip()
# TODO: print s reversed

const s = require('fs').readFileSync(0, 'utf8').trim();
// TODO: print s reversed

import java.util.*;
public class Main { public static void main(String[] a){ Scanner sc=new Scanner(System.in); String s=sc.next(); /* TODO */ } }

hints.text: Two pointers — one at the start, one at the end.

problems.editorialHtml: Swap from both ends toward the middle, or use a built-in reverse.

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.statementHtml: Given a string of brackets ()[]{}, print true if every bracket is correctly matched and nested, else false.

starters.code:
s = input().strip()
# TODO: print 'true' or 'false'

const s = require('fs').readFileSync(0, 'utf8').trim();
// TODO: print 'true' or 'false'

import java.util.*;
public class Main { public static void main(String[] a){ Scanner sc=new Scanner(System.in); String s=sc.next(); /* TODO */ } }

hints.text: Push opening brackets; on a closing bracket, the top of the stack must be its match.
At the end the stack must be empty.

problems.title: Maximum Subarray Sum
problems.statementHtml:
Given an array of n integers, find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) with the largest sum and print that sum.

Input
Line 1: n.
Line 2: n integers.

starters.code:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().split()
n = int(data[0])
arr = list(map(int, data[1:1+n]))
# TODO: print the maximum subarray sum

import java.util.*;
public class Main { public static void main(String[] g){ Scanner sc=new Scanner(System.in); int n=sc.nextInt(); long[] a=new long[n]; for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=sc.nextLong(); /* TODO */ } }

hints.text: Track the best sum ending exactly at the current index.
cur = max(a[i], cur + a[i]); best = max(best, cur). This is Kadane's algorithm.

problems.editorialHtml: Kadane's algorithm: O(n) time, O(1) space. Reset the running sum whenever it would hurt you.

solutions.code:
import sys
d=sys.stdin.read().split()
n=int(d[0]); a=list(map(int,d[1:1+n]))
cur=best=a[0]
for x in a[1:]:
    cur=max(x,cur+x); best=max(best,cur)
print(best)

problems.title: Binary Search Index
problems.statementHtml:
Given a sorted array and a target, print the 0-based index of the target, or -1 if absent.

Input
Line 1: n and target.
Line 2: n sorted integers.
problems.constraintsHtml:
• 1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
  • Array is sorted ascending, values distinct.

starters.code:
import sys
d = sys.stdin.read().split()
n = int(d[0]); target = int(d[1])
arr = list(map(int, d[2:2+n]))
# TODO: print index of target or -1

import java.util.*;
public class Main { public static void main(String[] g){ Scanner sc=new Scanner(System.in); int n=sc.nextInt(); long t=sc.nextLong(); long[] a=new long[n]; for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=sc.nextLong(); /* TODO */ } }

hints.text: Maintain lo/hi bounds and compare the midpoint to the target.

solutions.code:
import sys, bisect
d=sys.stdin.read().split()
n=int(d[0]); t=int(d[1]); a=list(map(int,d[2:2+n]))
i=bisect.bisect_left(a,t)
print(i if i<n and a[i]==t else -1)

problems.title: High Earning Employees
problems.statementHtml: Table employees(id, name, salary). Return the name and salary of every employee earning more than 50000, ordered by salary descending.

sql.schemaSql:
CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, salary INTEGER);
INSERT INTO employees (id, name, salary) VALUES
  (1, 'Alice', 72000),
  (2, 'Bob', 48000),
  (3, 'Carol', 95000),
  (4, 'Dan', 50000),
  (5, 'Eve', 51000);
sql.solutionSql: SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000 ORDER BY salary DESC;

hints.text: Filter with WHERE, then ORDER BY salary DESC.

problems.editorialHtml: SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000 ORDER BY salary DESC;
